topic: barrel use and lees contact in full-bodied white wine
trigger_keywords: uso de barrica y contacto con lias

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A white wine is fermented or aged in barrel while remaining in contact with its fine lees.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Oak contributes flavour and gradual oxygen exposure, while lees autolysis releases mannoproteins and polysaccharides that increase palate weight and texture.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The combined treatment produces a fuller, rounder white wine with greater textural and aromatic complexity.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The combined treatment produces a fuller, rounder white wine with greater textural and aromatic complexity.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
